shower.thought: We spend the first part of life learning to be ourselves, and the second part unlearning what we learned.
If you've never been bored, you've never really thought about anything.
The only time you're truly present is when you've forgotten to check the time.
You can't prove you're conscious, which means you can't prove anyone else isn't.
Nostalgia is just homesickness for a time you can never actually return to, no matter how hard you try.
We call it "falling in love" because there's no net to catch you if it goes wrong.
Every conversation you have with a stranger is a story they'll never fully tell anyone else.
The best teachers are usually the ones who remember being confused.
You only know what food tastes like by comparing it to other food you've eaten, so your first meal was your only pure experience of taste.
Money is just a collective agreement to value something invisible.
The things you're most afraid of saying are probably the things someone else is desperate to hear.
You've probably had the same thought as millions of people, but never at the same exact moment, which makes you lonelier than you think.
Success often feels empty because you spent so long chasing it that you forgot why you started.
The older you get, the faster time moves, which means life actually accelerates toward its end.
A person with nothing to lose is the most dangerous and most free person in the room.
You can't remember most of your life, which means you're already a ghost of who you used to be.
The world makes sense until you ask why, and then it falls apart.
We fear the unknown, but the known is just the unknown we've gotten used to.
Forgiveness isn't about the other person—it's about releasing the person they made you become.
Your biggest fear and your greatest strength are often the same thing.

motivational.thought: Your potential is not something you find. It's something you build. — Unknown
The best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago. The second best time is now. — Ancient Proverb
Comfort is the enemy of growth. Choose the harder path. — Unknown
You're not behind. You're not ahead. You're exactly where you need to be to learn what comes next. — Unknown
Discipline is choosing what you want most over what you want now. — Unknown
The person you'll be in five years is determined by the books you read and the people you surround yourself with today. — Unknown
Comparison is the thief of contentment and the killer of ambition. Pick one and commit. — Unknown
Your past doesn't define you. Your next decision does. — Unknown
The only real failure is the failure to try. Everything else is just data. — Unknown
Start before you're ready. Readiness is a myth we tell ourselves to avoid the work. — Unknown
What lies behind you and what lies before you pale in comparison to what lies within you. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
Every scar is proof you survived something that tried to break you. Wear it. — Unknown
The world doesn't need another person who knows what to do. It needs someone willing to actually do it. — Unknown
You don't rise to the level of your goals. You fall to the level of your systems. — Unknown
Small progress is still progress. A river carves a canyon one drop at a time. — Unknown
The only permission you need is your own. Stop waiting for someone else to believe in you first. — Unknown
You can spend your life climbing the ladder only to realize it's leaning against the wrong wall. Choose your wall carefully. — Unknown
Excellence is not a destination. It's a direction. — Unknown
Your future self is watching you right now, waiting to see if you're going to do what it takes. — Unknown
The gap between who you are and who you want to be is filled with the work you're willing to do. — Unknown

darkjoke.thought:
I told my therapist I was afraid of commitment.
She said, "Let's book you for the same time next week," and suddenly I was terrified.

My friend asked if I believed in ghosts.
I said, "Not until the credit card bill for my ex's funeral showed up."

I went to a self-help seminar on confidence.
The speaker said, "Act like you belong anywhere." I'm now banned from three country clubs and a courthouse.

My dad asked why I wasn't married yet.
I said, "I'm waiting for the right person." He said, "At this rate, you'll be waiting for someone who's already married to someone else."

I told my friend he had unrealistic expectations about life.
He said, "Says the guy who still thinks his parents are proud of him."

My doctor told me I was overweight.
I said, "Second opinion?" He said, "You're also ugly."

I asked my boss if I could work from home.
He said, "Sure, as soon as you prove you can work from the office."

My therapist asked about my childhood.
I said, "It was fine." She said, "That explains so much."

I tried to join a gym.
The trainer said, "First, tell me your fitness goals." I said, "To not be here." He said, "Then you're already succeeding."

My friend said he was "living his best life."
I asked where. He said, "In his imagination." I said, "That's the saddest part—he's still unhappy there."

I told my mom I was becoming a minimalist.
She said, "That's just what poor people call it."

My coworker asked if I was okay.
I said, "Yeah, why?" He said, "You've been staring at your spreadsheet for an hour without moving." I said, "It's either meditation or a cry for help." He said, "It's showing."

I asked my friend if he had any regrets.
He said, "Just one." I said, "What?" He said, "Having this conversation with you."

My therapist asked if I was suicidal.
I said, "Only on weekdays." She said, "That's progress. Last week it was weekdays and weekends."

I told my date I had a five-year plan.
She asked what it was. I said, "Make it to six years." She said, "That's the most honest thing anyone's said to me, and I hate it."

My friend said, "I think I'm depressed."
I said, "Have you tried being happy?" He said, "That's not how it works." I said, "I know—I've been trying for years."

I asked my barber how he stays positive.
He said, "I don't—I just cut hair and judge people quietly." Finally, someone being honest.

My mom asked if I was seeing anyone.
I said, "Just my therapist and my creditors. Them I see constantly."

I told my friend he should be more optimistic.
He said, "Easy for you to say." I said, "What do you mean?" He said, "You're delusional—that's basically the same thing."

My doctor said I needed to exercise more.
I said, "I walk to my car." He said, "That's a start." I said, "From the couch."
